items.title: Solving Thundering Herds with Request Coalescing in Go
items.summary: Caches are a wonderful way to make your most frequent operations cheaper. If you’ve got a resource somewhere on disk (or a network hop away) that is accessed often, changes infrequently, and fits in memory, you’ve got an excellent candidate for a cache! Caching Celebrity Posts For example, consider a social media post from a famous celebrity. This celebrity…

items.title: Enum vs Enum Class in C++
items.summary: In C++, enumerations (enums) are a powerful tool for creating named sets of integer constants. They help improve code readability and maintainability by providing meaningful names to numeric values. However, C++ offers two ways to define enums: the traditional enum and the more modern enum class. In this blog, we’ll explore the differences between these two…

items.title: Let vs Var vs Const in JavaScript
items.summary: JavaScript is a versatile and widely used programming language that offers multiple ways to declare variables, including let , var , and const . Each of these declarations serves a specific purpose and has its own scope and behavior. In this blog post, we’ll dive into the differences between let , var , and const in JavaScript with examples to help you unde…

items.title: "AI", and the trouble with inaccessible SaaS
items.summary: I wasn't particularly enthused by the alpha release of Vercel's v0 UI generation tool this week. Provided with a prompt, it produces React code with Tailwind to style it. It's being marketed as a prototyping tool, but was described by Vercel's CEO on Twitter as "production-grade" . Well, which one is it? To be clear, my issue isn't with the concept of large…

items.title: Satun
items.summary: I jump on the ferry, five minutes walk from the marina to complete my obligatory visa run. Less than 90 minutes takes me to Satun in Thailand. Against all the odds the hotel staff are waiting for me at the terminal with my car. Next day I head off to PSS shipyard to see Des and Ked Kearns who are just about to finish the rebuild of Cariad. items.title: To rent or to sell, that is the question
items.summary: As a real estate developer, one of the big decisions you need to make is whether you will rent or sell the buildings you've built. Income from rentals flows in steadily over years, while income from sales hits all at once. This essential difference is simple but has many implications for your risk profile, upside potential, capital requirements, and busines…

items.title: Vacuum tube nostalgia
items.summary: Music enthusiasts will tell you there's nothing quite like "tube sound", the warm, rich sound quality that old vacuum tube amplifiers bring to the music played through them. items.title: The silence is deafening
items.summary: Imagine you're at a dinner party, and you're getting into a heated argument. As you start yelling, the other people quickly hush their voices and start glaring at you. items.title: Urban sprawl is a tragedy of the commons
items.summary: We tend to choose larger homes than we want our neighbors to choose. The result: sprawling development that doesn't match what people actually want from their communities. items.title: Remote work will break the US monopoly on global talent
items.summary: Remote work is creating a new economic niche, and countries that put in the work to fill it will finally be able to compete with the US to attract talent. This is the once-in-a-generation opportunity for small, stable countries to grow, diversify, and up-skill their economy. items.title: Uncharted waters: what startups can learn from pirates about compensation

Just like many startups, they also balanced equity incentives with other mechanisms that would be familiar to a startup employee today, such as bonuses. items.title: Inflation propagates unevenly
items.summary: Inflation hits some parts of the economy harder and faster than others. It's obvious once you say it, and yet the way pundits and academics talk about inflation glosses over this reality. items.title: Israel's water miracle
items.summary: The saga of Israeli water management is one of the most inspiring stories of the last 75 years. Israel went from having a serious water problem to being a net water exporter to neighboring countries. Meanwhile, the population grew from ~1 million to well over 9 million. items.title: Quickly make any LÖVE app programmable from within the app
